title: Canonical Work
description: A canonical work record — the abstract intellectual entity (FRBR Work). Distinct from Manuscript (FRBR Manifestation+Item). Phase 0 forward-declared; activation in P0.2 with the OpenITI seed (~13,681 files mapped to work PIDs) and source-namespace ETL (every Bosworth/Yâqūt/Le Strange edition becomes a work record). Bibliographic citations from provenance.derived_from migrate from CURIE strings to work PIDs in P0.2.

labels.description: Title bundle. prefLabel.ar typically holds the canonical Arabic title; prefLabel.en the conventional English title (when one exists).

composition_temporal.description: When the work was composed. Often only approximate; use approximation='circa' for round-century dates and 'before' / 'after' for terminus dates.

original_language.description: BCP-47 language tag of the original composition. Common: 'ar', 'fa', 'ota', 'tr'. Translations are separate work records cross-linked via 'translation_of'.

genre.description: Genre classification. Controlled vocabulary based on classical Islamic bibliographic categories (Kâtib Çelebi's Kashf al-Ẓunūn taxonomy as reference). Multi-valued (a work may be both 'tarikh' and 'tabaqa' — biographical chronicle).

subjects.description: Free-text subject keywords (Library-of-Congress style). For controlled subject access in P0.3+ via skos:Concept.

extant_manuscripts.description: Known surviving manuscript witnesses. PID references to iac:manuscript-. Bidirectional with manuscript.witnesses_work.

translation_of.description: If this work is a translation, the original work PID. Translations are first-class works with their own authors (translators).

abridgement_of.description: If this is a mukhtaṣar (abridgement), the source work PID.

commentary_on.description: If this is a sharḥ or ḥāshiyah (commentary or supercommentary), the source work PID.

mentions_places.description: Places named in the work — important for geographical/biographical works (Yâqūt, Ibn Baṭṭūṭa, Le Strange). Populated by NER pipelines.

cites_works.description: Other works explicitly cited or quoted. Important for intellectual-history graph.

openiti_uri.description: OpenITI canonical URI ('<deathYearAH>AuthorShortname.WorkShortname'). Phase 0.2 active reconciliation target.

bosworth_id.description: Bosworth NID — used when the work IS Bosworth's NID for a particular dynasty. Most works have no bosworth_id.

$comment: Phase 0.2 hard rules: (a) authors required (anonymous works use a sentinel iac:person-00000000 'Anonymous'); (b) original_language required; (c) at least one of composition_temporal or floruit-of-author required.
